clinical_reasoning: 4-year-old female presenting with fever (39.8°C) and rash appearing this morning, with associated lethargy and anorexia. Tachycardia (HR 185) is expected with high fever in this age group. Blood pressure 85/50 and SpO2 96% are within acceptable pediatric ranges. Respiratory rate 34 is mildly elevated but not concerning. Standard pediatric fever assessment: the child is alert, febrile, and has a rash that needs characterization. Most common cause at this age is viral exanthem. ORANGE triage level — requires timely physician assessment to evaluate rash morphology and exclude serious infections.
vital_sign_concerns: HR 185 — tachycardia in context of fever
BP 85/50 — lower end of normal for age
